title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective trusted extension API and controller contract; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in crds, controllers, operators, admission policies, webhooks, and extension trust, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in trusted extension API and controller contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.
Build or evaluate controllers from watches, work queues, level-based reconciliation, ownership, idempotency, finalizers, status, retries, leader election, and rate limits.
Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.

Inspect the K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1oj54v". Tailor its extension-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Extension Upgrade Trust into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, keep K8s Extension Upgrade Trust connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1oj54v
  - competency: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: extension upgrade trust
    environment: clean conformance cluster with only required add-ons installed
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: scheduler event eligible domains skew and placement result
    case-key: 1oj54v
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Extension Upgrade Trust structural check is only one gate. trusted extension API and controller contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Extension Upgrade Trust precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Deleting an operator safely removes all custom resources, external state, and admission dependencies.
